response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems software developer with ~17 years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, backend, and embedded software engineering. He has no discernible experience in SoC or IP modeling, GPU/AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, or deep neural network training and inference optimization — which are the core competencies this role demands at a principal level. His recent AI-adjacent work (MCP frameworks, agentic tooling) is software infrastructure, not hardware performance engineering, and does not bridge the gap to the hardware-software co-design expertise required here.
strengths: Sustained career (~17 years) showing consistent technical growth and adaptability across diverse platforms including embedded and low-level systems
Demonstrated performance-sensitive software engineering experience (Sociomantic: high-throughput microservices, async I/O, low-latency DLang applications at millions of req/s)
Hardware-adjacent exposure including C/C++ components for National Instruments measurement tooling and robotic arm software
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience — the foundational technical requirement of this role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator hardware background of any kind
Education consists of two bachelor's degrees with no advanced degree; role prefers PhD or Master's for principal-level hardware architecture work
